page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It is configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture and is paired with 96.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU core, aligning with compute-intensive design standards.

This instance does not include bundled local NVMe SSD storage or integrated GPU hardware accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The c4a-highcpu-48 is designed for high-density compute workloads where memory footprint requirements are secondary to raw processing power. By offering a lean 2.0 GB of RAM per core, it provides a cost-efficient hardware profile for parallel processing tasks that do not require massive memory arrays. Suitable workloads include high-performance computing, batch processing, compute-heavy web serving, and distributed analytics.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance from the c4a family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence, and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is designed for compute-heavy workloads that require substantial processing power without the overhead of a large memory footprint. The lean memory-to-CPU ratio makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for highly parallelized tasks. Appropriate workloads for this instance include batch processing, distributed compute tasks, and high-performance web serving.
